name: Real Estate
description: Cinematic property showcase: 20-60s tour built from 4-10 keyframes that translate bricks into aspiration. Uses photoreal flythroughs, golden-hour exteriors, threshold reveals, and view-anchored interior moves to sell the lifestyle, not the floor plan. Reference-required: real property photos must be supplied (no AI-improvised real estate — buyers spot fake instantly). Works for residential listings, luxury, vacation rentals, commercial, new construction, and renovation before/after. Vertical 9:16 cuts for IG/TikTok, 16:9 master for Zillow/YouTube/agent websites.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter, multi-ref WITH user-supplied property photos (required for any frame depicting a specific real property — exterior, signature view, named room). 4-10 keyframes spanning the narrative arc: exterior establish → entrance → 2-4 interior anchors → outdoor / view → twilight final.
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, 5s clips. Most cuts are slow camera moves — drone push-in, gimbal walkthrough, window approach, threshold reveal. generate_audio: false; sound is layered in composition.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — optional. When present: warm, low-pressure agent or owner narration with concrete spec callouts (sqft, bedrooms, view). Many high-end listings work better silent + music + on-screen specs.
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1; MinimalCaptions or LowerThirdCaptions for property specs (address, beds/baths, sqft, price). Avoid loud Hormozi style — wrong register for luxury real estate.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — single track, ambient or contemporary classical for luxury, warm acoustic for cozy/cabin, jazz-inflected electronic for urban modern. Builds toward final view reveal. Two-part track for renovation before/after (tense → resolved).

constants.structure: exterior arrival → threshold → primary living → kitchen / view → bedroom / bath → outdoor or twilight final
constants.pacingRule: every camera move slow — pan crosses room in 8-12s not 3s
constants.viewBudget: if property has a view, 15-20% of total runtime is view-on-screen

variationAxes: property archetype (luxury-waterfront / urban-modern / cozy-family / rustic-cabin / commercial-office / vacation-rental / historic / new-construction / renovation-before-after / land-and-lot)
time of day (golden-hour / bright-daylight / twilight-blue-hour / warm-evening / day-to-night-transition)
camera language (drone-aerial / steadicam-walkthrough / dolly-glide / orbit / threshold-reveal / window-approach)
duration cut (20-30s social-teaser / 45-60s reel-tour / 3-5min full-listing — pick one and commit)
doNotCopyLiterally: Specific property layout, agent script, exact spec numbers, brand markers visible in references. The pattern (exterior → threshold → views → twilight) and pacing rules are reusable; the property itself must come from the user's photos. AI-improvised real estate is a defect — never invent a layout the user didn't supply.
referenceNotes: Property photos are REQUIRED. The reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) refuses if no real estate reference exists at workspace/projects/<id>/assets/uploaded/. Minimum: 1 exterior photo + 1 signature interior. Recommended: full set (exterior approach, foyer, living, kitchen, primary bedroom, primary bath, signature view, outdoor). For a known address, MLS or listing-site photos work as reference. Generating without reference produces generic stock-looking interiors that read as fake to buyers and tank conversion.
